basics.name: John Loy
basics.label: Web Software Engineer
basics.summary: During my time designing and building for the web over the past 25 years, I have worn the hats of Webmaster, UX designer, back-end engineer, front-end engineer, and technical lead. My past and current professional roles demanded I continually adapt my skills to meet each new challenge, whether that entailed designing, coding, architecting, leading development teams, or working with clients and stakeholders. Lately, I might call myself a front-leaning full-stack engineer, focused on a React + TypeScript + Node stack, but I prefer to just call myself versatile and experienced.

work.name: Dreamworks Animation, NBC Universal
work.summary: Developed custom web applications to support the data management needs of animators and other specialists involved in film production at the Dreamworks Animation film studio.
highlights: Developed and maintained a web-based file transfer tool to facilitate safely exchanging film assets between Dreamworks and vendors
Developed and maintained a web-based tool for inspecting the readiness of 3D model assets, including a WebGL-based 3D model viewer
Developed and maintained a web-based digital "backlot" tool for retrieving and reusing digital assets used in past Dreamworks films
Assisted with development and management of a visual design system of UI components for use in Dreamworks web-based applications

work.name: WillowTree
work.summary: Architected, developed, and advised in a senior client-facing capacity to produce web applications for multi-platform client projects.
highlights: Developed the first version of the https://sigmasquared.io/ HR tool for talent optimization, providing D3.js-based visualizations for data analysis
Developed features for an internal supply chain management application for Anheuser-Busch InBev using React, TypeScript, Docker, Node (API), SQL Server, and Azure
Migrated a Chrome/Firefox web extension to Safari for Capital One using Angular, TypeScript, and XCode
Led an initiative to migrate the WillowTree website from a React SSR SPA to a static JAMStack site using a custom SSG build tool built on standard Web Components and Netlify
Mentored colleagues at WillowTree, particularly about web standards and the web platform
Used a variety of front-end frameworks and technologies, including React, Angular, Web Components, and TypeScript

work.name: WillowTree
work.summary: Architected, developed, and advised in a senior client-facing capacity to produce web applications for multi-platform client projects.
highlights: Led development of a full rewrite of the HBO Nordic OTT streaming web application, a React+Redux+TypeScript SPA, from inception to release and maintenance
Developed features for the HBO Nordic and Fox Chromecast applications
Provided on-site support for the HBO Nordic client
Mentored colleagues, new hires, and interns at WillowTree

work.name: Roomkey
work.summary: Developed the UI of a hotel booking meta search website.
highlights: Developed the centerpiece autocomplete search UI involving live search filtering, search results, and geo results visualization
Worked with Backbone.js on the front-end
Worked with Clojure on the back-end

work.name: Scitent
work.summary: Developed custom LMS solutions, primarily for the health care industry.
highlights: Executed a rewrite in Rails of the flagship Scitent web-based learning management system, tailored to clients like the American Heart Association
Worked with Backbone.js and Knockout.js on the front-end
Worked with Ruby on Rails and MySQL on the back-end

work.name: Intalgent
work.summary: Developed the UI of bespoke web application solutions in a small agency setting.
highlights: Served clients from e-commerce, higher education, non-profit, and medical industries
Developed the Youth Leadership Initiative for the U.Va. Center for Politics, a mock election web application serving tens of thousands of educators and students worldwide
Worked with jQuery, and otherwise “vanilla” JavaScript, on the front-end
Worked with PHP, Rails, Grails, and ColdFusion on the back-end

work.name: University of Virginia Library
work.summary: Stewarded the web presence of the U.Va. Library as part of the library communications team.
highlights: Guided the library through a redesign effort, involving information architecture, visual design, SEO, technical solutions, and stakeholder training
Developed a UI framework, templating, and CMS solution to accommodate the needs of thousands of pages and dozens of website stakeholders
Contributed to the UI development of the library catalog search
Conducted user research to inform ongoing site development

work.name: University of Virginia, College and Graduate School of Arts & Sciences
work.summary: Stewarded the web presence of the U.Va. College and Graduate School of Arts & Sciences as part of the A&S communications team
highlights: Guided the College and Graduate School through a redesign effort, involving information architecture, visual design, SEO, technical solutions, and stakeholder training
Designed, built, and maintained the first two incarnations of A&S Online, the primary web news publication of the College and Graduate School of Arts & Sciences
Developed a custom CMS solution for A&S Online using PHP, MySQL, and Apache

work.name: Gibson Design Associates
highlights: Designed and assisted with production of print publications such as brochures, pamphlets, corporate identity packages, event invitations, and advertising displays
Designed layout and coded websites for corporate and non-profit clients, including Monticello and the University of Virginia
Produced illustrations as graphic elements for design projects

volunteer.summary: Participated in week-long Jimmy Carter Work Project building houses
Designed the EFN logo and website. Bagged food parcels weekly for distribution to local individuals and families suffering from food insecurity.
Provided live-in life assistance to adults with disabilities. Started computer skills program for residents.
Tutored elementary school students in reading

projects.name: Edui Conference
projects.description: A conference for web professionals in higher education
